tx_552_disclosure_required.description: Texas Chapter 552 disclosure applies because the system is used for covered consumer interaction or healthcare service/treatment and no PHI/IIHI exception blocks disclosure

tx_552_privacy_exception.description: PHI / IIHI confidentiality exception limits Chapter 552 disclosure

tx_552_healthcare_recipient.description: Healthcare-service disclosure must be given to the service recipient or the recipient's personal representative

obligations.content: Disclose the use of the artificial intelligence system even when that use would otherwise be obvious.
Make the disclosure clear and conspicuous, in plain language, and without dark patterns.
The required disclosure may be provided through a hyperlink to a separate webpage containing the disclosure.
For emergency care, provide the disclosure as soon as reasonably possible.
Outside emergency care, provide the disclosure no later than the date the service or treatment is first provided.
For healthcare service or treatment, provide the disclosure to the recipient of the service or treatment or the recipient's personal representative.
Treat protected health information or individually identifiable health information as a confidentiality-based exception or limitation to Chapter 552 disclosure.

notes: This rule is independently evaluated from the biometric privacy, general AI deployment, and practitioner diagnostic-use rules.
The PHI / IIHI issue is surfaced as an exception or limitation rather than ignored.
